title: Item column value to subitem column value
excerpt: I have a board that has a column “attorney”. In the subitems, I want to set a formula and filters based on the attorney name. Therefore, I want to copy the attorney value to a subitem column (when item is created and whe…

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a board that has a column “attorney”. In the subitems, I want to set a formula and filters based on the attorney name. Therefore, I want to copy the attorney value to a subitem column (when item is created and when attorney column changes).
Is there any way to do this?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Soroh,

Is it possible for you to use the connect board and mirror column to connect the parent item and column values into the subitems? You could then pull that connected/mirrored data into a formula column! What do you think?

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to do this automatically when a subitem is created?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I am afraid not at this time - this would need to be a manual task. I apologise for the setback! Please feel free to submit this as a feature request

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Soroh,

We developed an app that solves this exact pain point of subitems inheriting column value from parent. It’s called Subitem Automations and it allows to do this on subitem creation and when the parent item changes, here’s an example -

On creation - Populate item’s data into its subitems in a seamless way

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.31.152226×400 30.1 KB

On change - Being able to set dependency between a subitem to an item

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.30.162106×432 27.6 KB

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - monday.com: Apps Marketplace

Hope that helps!

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

You can do that easily with our Subitems Pro Automations app. It includes a automations that allows you to copy an item’s column value to a selected column in all its subitems.

  1. When column changes

    image334×260 8.36 KB

  2. When subitem is created

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!

posts.author: i-jaglan (Sagar Jaglan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Soroh and others looking for this functionality,

We built SubItems Pro specifically to solve this challenge of inheriting column values from parent items to subitems.

What SubItems Pro offers:

Value Inheritance - When creating subitems, automatically inherit:

  • Status values
  • Person/Owner assignments
  • Date & Timeline values
  • Number column values

Bulk Operations - Create 1-50 subitems at once across multiple selected items (works with monday.com’s multi-select feature)

Clone with Values - Clone existing subitems from a source item to multiple target items, with the option to include all column values

How it works:

  1. Select multiple items on your board
  2. Choose which values to inherit from parent items
  3. Create subitems in bulk - all values transfer automatically

This is especially useful when you need consistent subitem structure across many items without manually copying values each time.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace: Subitem Pro

Happy to answer any questions!
